title: I plant a tree
summary: An old man plants a small tree he knows he will not see grow tall.
body: I am old now. My back hurts. My eyes are not strong. But today, I planted a tree in my garden. It is a small oak. It is not taller than my knee. People in my family ask me, 'Why? You will not see it grow tall.' I smile. I do not answer fast. The tree is for my grandchildren. The tree is also for the birds. Birds do not know my name. They will sit on the tree one day. They will sing. I will not hear them. But the tree will be there. The sun is warm now. I drink some water. I look at the small oak. I think, 'You will be a big tree. I will be a small memory. That is good.'

annotations.meaningVi: một ngày nào đó (trong tương lai)
sẽ vẫn ở đó / sẽ tồn tại ở đó

grammarNotes.title: Future with 'will'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'will' để nói về điều sẽ xảy ra trong tương lai: 'They will sit', 'I will not hear', 'You will be a big tree'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'will' for the future: 'They will sit', 'I will not hear them', 'You will be a big tree'.

People in my family ask me, '{{b5}}? You will not see it grow tall.' I smile. I do not answer fast. The tree is {{b6}} my grandchildren. The tree is also for the birds. Birds do not know my {{b7}}. They will sit on the tree one day. They will sing. I will not {{b8}} them. But the tree will be there. The sun is warm now. I {{b9}} some water. I look at the small oak. translationVi: Tôi giờ đã già. Lưng tôi đau. Mắt tôi không còn tinh. Nhưng hôm nay, tôi đã trồng một cái cây trong vườn. Đó là một cây sồi nhỏ. Nó chưa cao bằng đầu gối tôi. Người trong nhà hỏi tôi: 'Sao thế? Ông sẽ không nhìn thấy nó lớn đâu.' Tôi mỉm cười. Tôi không trả lời ngay. Cái cây này là dành cho các cháu của tôi. Cái cây cũng dành cho lũ chim. Lũ chim không biết tên tôi. Một ngày nào đó chúng sẽ đậu trên cành. Chúng sẽ hót. Tôi sẽ không nghe được. Nhưng cái cây sẽ vẫn còn ở đó. Mặt trời ấm áp. Tôi uống một ngụm nước. Tôi nhìn cây sồi nhỏ. Tôi nghĩ: 'Mày sẽ thành một cây to. Tao sẽ thành một ký ức nhỏ. Như thế là tốt rồi.'

questions.hint: Look for the kind of tree.

questions.prompt: How tall is the tree now?
questions.explanation: 'It is not taller than my knee.'

questions.prompt: What do people in the family ask?
options: Why? You will not see it grow tall.
questions.explanation: Family members ask why he plants a tree he won't see grow.
questions.hint: Look for a question in the passage.

questions.explanation: 'The tree is for my grandchildren. The tree is also for the birds.'

options: No, they don't know his name
questions.hint: What does it say about names?

questions.prompt: Will the writer hear the birds sing on his tree?
options: No, he will not hear them

questions.prompt: How does the writer feel about being a 'small memory'?
questions.hint: Look at the very last words.

questions.prompt: What is the weather like in the story?

options: Doing something good for the future is enough, even if you do not see it
questions.explanation: He plants for a future he will not see, and is content with that.
questions.hint: Why is he happy to plant a tree he won't see grow?

criticalThinkingQuestion: The old man does something good for people he will never meet. Have you ever received a gift from someone you never met — and could you give one in return to a stranger in the future?
